# resample { #neuroim.resample }

`resample`

Resampling and reorientation functions for neuroimaging data.

This module provides functions for resampling and reorienting neuroimaging
volumes and vectors to match different spatial configurations.

## Functions

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [reorient](#neuroim.resample.reorient) | Remap the grid-to-world coordinates mapping of an image. |
| [resample](#neuroim.resample.resample) | Resample an image to match the space of another image. |
| [resample_to](#neuroim.resample.resample_to) | Resample a volume or vector to a target using neuroim2-style names. |
| [resample_vec](#neuroim.resample.resample_vec) | Resample a 4D NeuroVec to match the space of another image. |

### reorient { #neuroim.resample.reorient }

```python
resample.reorient(x, orient)
```

Remap the grid-to-world coordinates mapping of an image.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type                                                                                       | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Default    |
|--------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| x      | [NeuroSpace](`neuroim.neuro_space.NeuroSpace`) or [NeuroVol](`neuroim.neuro_vol.NeuroVol`) | The object to reorient                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | _required_ |
| orient | str or list of str                                                                         | The orientation code indicating the remapped axes. Can be a 3-letter string like "RAS" or list like ["R", "A", "S"] Each position represents: - First: "R" (Right) or "L" (Left) - Second: "A" (Anterior) or "P" (Posterior) - Third: "S" (Superior) or "I" (Inferior) | _required_ |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                                                                       | Description               |
|--------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------|
|        | [NeuroSpace](`neuroim.neuro_space.NeuroSpace`) or [NeuroVol](`neuroim.neuro_vol.NeuroVol`) | A reoriented version of x |

#### R Equivalent {.doc-section .doc-section-r-equivalent}

neuroim2::reorient

#### Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> # Reorient a space to RAS
>>> space_ras = reorient(space, "RAS")
>>>
>>> # Or using a list
>>> space_ras = reorient(space, ["R", "A", "S"])
```

### resample { #neuroim.resample.resample }

```python
resample.resample(source, target, interpolation=3)
```

Resample an image to match the space of another image.

This function resamples a source image to match the spatial properties
(dimensions, resolution, and orientation) of a target image or space.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name          | Type                                                                                       | Description                                                                                           | Default    |
|---------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| source        | [NeuroVol](`neuroim.neuro_vol.NeuroVol`)                                                   | The source volume to be resampled                                                                     | _required_ |
| target        | [NeuroVol](`neuroim.neuro_vol.NeuroVol`) or [NeuroSpace](`neuroim.neuro_space.NeuroSpace`) | The target space to match the dimensions and orientation                                              | _required_ |
| interpolation | [int](`int`)                                                                               | Type of interpolation to be applied: - 0: nearest neighbor - 1: trilinear - 3: cubic spline (default) | `3`        |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                               | Description                                                           |
|--------|----------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [DenseNeuroVol](`neuroim.neuro_vol.DenseNeuroVol`) | The resampled source image with the same spatial properties as target |

#### Raises {.doc-section .doc-section-raises}

| Name   | Type                         | Description                        |
|--------|------------------------------|------------------------------------|
|        | [ImportError](`ImportError`) | If nibabel is not installed        |
|        | [ValueError](`ValueError`)   | If interpolation is not 0, 1, or 3 |

#### R Equivalent {.doc-section .doc-section-r-equivalent}

neuroim2::resample

#### Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> # Resample to match another volume
>>> resampled = resample(source_vol, target_vol, interpolation=1)
>>>
>>> # Resample to match a specific space
>>> new_space = NeuroSpace(dim=[128, 128, 64], spacing=[2, 2, 2])
>>> resampled = resample(source_vol, new_space)
```

### resample_to { #neuroim.resample.resample_to }

```python
resample.resample_to(source, target, method='linear', engine='nibabel')
```

Resample a volume or vector to a target using neuroim2-style names.

### resample_vec { #neuroim.resample.resample_vec }

```python
resample.resample_vec(source, target, interpolation=3)
```

Resample a 4D NeuroVec to match the space of another image.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name          | Type                                                                                       | Description                                             | Default    |
|---------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| source        | [NeuroVec](`neuroim.neuro_vec.NeuroVec`)                                                   | The source 4D vector to be resampled                    | _required_ |
| target        | [NeuroVec](`neuroim.neuro_vec.NeuroVec`) or [NeuroSpace](`neuroim.neuro_space.NeuroSpace`) | The target space to match (must be 4D if NeuroSpace)    | _required_ |
| interpolation | [int](`int`)                                                                               | Type of interpolation (0: nearest, 1: linear, 3: cubic) | `3`        |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                               | Description                 |
|--------|----------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------|
|        | [DenseNeuroVec](`neuroim.neuro_vec.DenseNeuroVec`) | The resampled source vector |

#### R Equivalent {.doc-section .doc-section-r-equivalent}

neuroim2::resample (for NeuroVec)
